critic_raw: The non-chronological blocked window evaluation is a departure from standard time-series evaluation practice. This is suspicious because departures from standard practice in evaluation design can be motivated by a desire to show more favorable results. The claim that residuals are near-i.i.d. at the 15-minute horizon needs empirical verification, not just assertion.
defender_raw: The claim is not merely asserted — it was empirically verified on six months of historical data before the final comparison. The residual autocorrelation analysis after conditioning on temperature, weekday, holiday, and lagged load showed near-zero dependence beyond two hours. A separate four-period comparison confirmed equivalent model rankings and near-identical MAPE between blocked random and rolling-origin evaluation. The evaluation plan pre-specified blocked windows based on those findings — this is the opposite of post-hoc cherry-picking of the favorable evaluation design.

debate_rounds.critic: The pre-analysis distinguishes this from post-hoc evaluation design selection. If the residual analysis was conducted before the final model comparison and the evaluation plan was pre-specified, the concern about motivated reasoning in evaluation design is resolved. I concede the main concern. The residual analysis is the key empirical justification and it was conducted prospectively.
debate_rounds.defender: Agreed. The pre-specification of the evaluation design based on empirical residual checks is the correct approach. This is analogous to using empirical power analysis to justify a sample size — you verify the assumption, then design the evaluation, then run it. The claim is also correctly limited to the short-horizon retrained task where the residual near-independence holds.

adjudication_raw: The critic fully concedes after one round when the prospective nature of the pre-analysis is acknowledged. Defense wins cleanly.
all_issues_raised: non-chronological split concern — conceded when prospective pre-analysis acknowledged

empirical_test.condition: Reproduce residual-dependence pre-analysis and four-period rolling-origin vs blocked-random comparison; verify pre-specification predates final model comparison
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Pre-analysis shows substantial residual dependence at 15-minute horizon, or four-period comparison reveals meaningful ranking differences
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Residual analysis confirms near-i.i.d. behavior at target horizon; rankings equivalent under both evaluation methods; pre-specification documented before final comparison
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Average results equivalent but edge cases reveal timing-dependent evaluation differences
